flashcards.definition: x(t) = A cos(ωt + φ); solving for t when x = x₀ requires inverse trig.
Occurs when the driving frequency equals the natural frequency, maximizing amplitude.
Points where displacement is always zero: sin(nπx/L) = 0 → x = kL/n.
ω = 2πf; relates to the period by T = 2π/ω.
Determines the initial position of the oscillator at t = 0.
